destinations.evidence: The payload path extracts the executor from the decoded async call and carries the same value into the detected call record.
The generated system async-commitment transaction receives the executor_address argument threaded from the decoded async call.
The transaction type stores executor_address as a first-class field initialized by its constructor.
destinations.snippet:
pub struct TxAsyncCommitment {
    ...
    pub executor_address: Address,
    pub commitment_validator: Address,
}
...
executor_address,

equivalence_argument: The current architecture renamed AsyncExec to TxAsyncCommitment, but the same generated system-transaction role is preserved: decoded.executor is passed into create_short_async_commitment_tx and stored as TxAsyncCommitment.executor_address.
notes: Mapped across the port payload path and Alloy transaction type.
rationale: The code now threads the already extracted executor_address into create_async_exec_transaction and into TxAsyncExec::new, while the added plan states the objective is to add a required executor address field for assignment and validation after simulation. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When an async call result creates its system TxAsyncExec transaction, the transaction must include the same executor_address extracted for that async call result.
suggested_falsification_tests: Construct or simulate an AsyncCallResult with a non-default executor address, inspect the produced system_tx as TxAsyncExec, and fail if tx.executor_address differs from the extracted executor_address.
summary: Async system transaction creation preserves the decoded executor address.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Simulate an async call with executor A and assert the generated TxAsyncCommitment.executor_address and DetectedShortAsyncCall.executor are A.
title: AsyncExec creation preserves executor address
